steps.title: Isolate the absolute value
steps.content:
Start with the equation:
$$-4|6x+5|+8=-12$$
Subtract $8$ and divide by $-4$:
$$-4|6x+5|=-20$$
$$|6x+5|=5$$

steps.title: Work through both cases
steps.content: An absolute value of $5$ means the inside can be $5$ or $-5$: $$6x+5=5 \quad \text{or} \quad 6x+5=-5$$ $$x=0 \quad \text{or} \quad x=-\frac{5}{3}$$ The answer is C.
